data.title: !NVADE SHOW!
data.title_japanese: !NVADE SHOW!
data.synopsis:
Animated music video for RAISE A SUILEN's song !NVADE SHOW!. It was first released on BanG Dream's official YouTube and was later included in the limited edition Blu-ray for RAISE A SUILEN's sixth single "mind of Prominence".

(Source: AniList)
This music video tells how a shy girl with a secret love and curiosity about what surrounds her works.

titles.title: "1-punkan dake Furete mo Ii yo..." Share House no Himitsu Rule.
「1分間だけ触れてもいいよ...」シェアハウスの秘密ルール.

data.title: "1-punkan dake Furete mo Ii yo..." Share House no Himitsu Rule.
data.title_japanese: 「1分間だけ触れてもいいよ...」シェアハウスの秘密ルール.
data.synopsis:
After moving into a new share house, Ryota Ochi meets two beautiful housemates…who order him to do housework day in and day out! He's soon had enough, and is just about to move out when the two girls offer him a deal—for every minute of housework he does, they allow him to "have fun" with them for one minute!

(Source: Suiseisha)

title_synonyms: "Please Describe Yourself in One Word": I'm Bad at the Question.
data.synopsis:
The anime depicts the "smart city" envisioned by Kyocera, where everyone can comfortably and safely live with the use of advanced technology, and it also depicts the worries of job seekers. The anime's title and theme reflect both the vexing issue of how to describe Kyocera's extensive business in just one word, and the mood of job seekers vexed by the interview question, "If you had to describe yourself in one word, what would it be?"

(Source: ANN)

data.synopsis: Episodes which depict the background stories of "Bungaku Shoujo" Movie's main heroines.
data.background: Each episode of "Bungaku Shoujo" Memoire received a limited one-week run in movie theaters in May 2010.

data.title: "Bungaku Shoujo" Movie
data.title_japanese: 劇場版“文学少女”
data.synopsis:
The protagonist of the story, Konoha Inoue, is a seemingly normal senior high 2nd year student. His high school life, other than a hinted incident 2 years ago, can be summed up as normal- if one can dismiss the secret fact that he used to be a female bestselling romance author. Due to that incident, however, he has now vowed never to write again.

This continued on until he was forced to join the literary club by the literary club president, the 3rd year female student Amano Tooko, a beautiful girl who has a taste for eating literary works. Now he has been tasked with writing her snack every day after school.

(Source: To Say Nothing of the Dog)
data.background: "Bungaku Shoujo" Movie was announced with the release of the first manga volume and the first light novel in April 2009. The first trailer was included with the release of the "Bungaku Shoujo" Kyou no Oyatsu: Hatsukoi DVD episode. The movie adapts the events of "Bungaku Shoujo" to Doukoku no Palmier, the fifth volume in the light novel series. It went on to earn 59 million yen in the Japanese box office.

data.title: "Calpis" Hakkou Monogatari
data.title_japanese: 「カルピス」発酵ものがたり
data.synopsis: A Calpis (Calpico in the US) commissioned short about the history of the company and how their product is made today. A few scenes use live-action footage. The short is animated by Takaharu Shimizu who used a Rube Goldberg style of story telling.

data.title: "Eiji"
data.title_japanese: 「エイジ」
data.synopsis:
Despite being the son of legendary boxer Keijirou Akagi, Eiji has no interest in boxing, preferring to play guitar in a punk band and brawl with his classmates. When his father's old coach scouts him, and his classmate Naoya Kusanagi becomes the high school boxing champion, his anger starts to boil up.

[Written by MAL Rewrite]

titles.title: “栄光なき天才たち”からの物語

data.title: "Eikou Naki Tensai-tachi" Kara no Monogatari
data.title_japanese: “栄光なき天才たち”からの物語
data.synopsis:
An anime/documentary series about athletes present by NHK.

The first episode adapts material from Tomoyoshi Itou and Shingo Morita's Weekly Young Jump biographical manga Eikou Naki Tensai-tachi (Geniuses Without Glory)—specifically the story about Hironoshin Furuhashi, the swimmer also known as "the flying fish of Fujiyama." The episode also includes interviews with Olympic swimmer Kousuke Kitajima and other Japanese swimmers about the how Furuhashi changed the Japanese competitive swimming world, as well as what sort of training is required to be an Olympic swimmer.

(Source: ANN, edited)

data.title: "Eiyuu" Kaitai
data.title_japanese: 「英雄」解体
data.synopsis:
Uro Yamada works as part of the "Hero Retirement and Return to Everyday Life Assistance Agency," which takes heroes from another world back to Earth and helps them reintegrate into everyday life.

(Source: ANN)

data.title: "Hataraku" no 100-nenshi
data.title_japanese: "働く"の100年史
data.synopsis:
It's been almost 100 years since the "salaryman" type of office worker first appeared in Japan back in the 1920s.

As our society faces a major turning point, SmartHR decided to create a brand film to reflect upon how work styles have changed with time over the past century.

We hope that by looking back on history, we inspire people to think about how they want to work going forward.

(Source: Official YouTube channel)

title_synonyms: I Don't Know Why but the Next Duke Who Said "I Have No Intention of Loving You" Is Doting on Me
data.synopsis:
Elsa, daughter of a fallen aristocrat, receives a marriage proposal from Julius, an elite aristocrat. "Why would such a noble man marry me?" Elsa was perplexed, but after the wedding, Julius, who until then had been kind and gentle, changed his mind!

(Source: Flex Comix, translated)

titles.title: 映画『キスできる餃子』×豆しば

data.title: "Kiss Dekiru Gyouza" Movie x Mameshiba
data.title_japanese: 映画『キスできる餃子』×豆しば
data.synopsis:
Two televised specials collaborating the Mameshiba franchise and the feature film Kiss Dekiru Gyoza being released June 2018. 

The film is about Yoko Fujita as a single mother who goes through a divorce. Afterwards, she returns to her parents' home and struggles to raise her child, also attempts to revive her parents' gyoza restaurant. She falls in love with pro golfer Ryo Iwahara.

The specials focus on shiba-ified Yoko and Ryo as they talked to Edamame. Special collaborative goods will be sold as well, including a bentou meal sold at specific train stations.

(Source: IMDB)

titles.title: "Omae Gotoki ga Maou ni Kateru to Omouna" to Yuusha Party wo Tsuihou sareta node, Outo de Kimama ni Kurashitai
Do You Think Someone Like You Can Defeat the Demon King?
Roll Over and Die: I Will Fight for an Ordinary Life with My Love and Cursed Sword!
「お前ごときが魔王に勝てると思うな」と勇者パーティを追放されたので、王都で気ままに暮らしたい

data.title: "Omae Gotoki ga Maou ni Kateru to Omouna" to Yuusha Party wo Tsuihou sareta node, Outo de Kimama ni Kurashitai
data.title_japanese: 「お前ごときが魔王に勝てると思うな」と勇者パーティを追放されたので、王都で気ままに暮らしたい
title_synonyms: Do You Think Someone Like You Can Defeat the Demon King?
Roll Over and Die: I Will Fight for an Ordinary Life with My Love and Cursed Sword!
data.synopsis:
Chosen by the Church's god, Origin, a young country girl named Flum Apricot is enlisted in the hero's party and expected to fight demons. However, her one innate skill, Reversal, has no practical use, and the party's sage, Jean Inteige, treats her as a burden, eventually selling her off to a slave dealer.

In the slave dealer's dark dungeon, all hope seems lost. But in a moment of desperation, Flum discovers that her skill actually turns the negative energy of cursed weapons into power, which helps her slay the slave owner and escape with another captured girl, Milkit. Now equipped with the means to fight and survive, Flum embraces the career of an adventurer and aims to lead a happy life with Milkit, away from the grim machinations of the Church.

[Written by MAL Rewrite]

data.title: "Parade" de Satie
data.title_japanese: サティの「パラード」
data.synopsis:
A Parade for three managers and four performers. 

(Source: Yamamura Animation)
data.background: French composer Erik Satie (1866-1925) created the ballet music piece "Parade" in 1916, at the age of 50, and it was first performed in 1917, with Jean Cocteau and Pablo Picasso taking part to its creation on the scene. The ballet was introduced by the poet Guillaume Apollinaire, who described it by using the term "surrealism" for the very first time. Mixing quotes from Satie’s essays with the music performed by a Dutch jazz band, the Willem Breuker Kollektief, this film is an animated re-creation of "realist ballet" images going beyond reality.
